name: task_no_user_facts
description: Transient task content with no durable user facts
content: Review Q3 budget with finance team — Sarah mentioned we need to cut 15% from the design tools line item. Check Figma enterprise vs team pricing.

name: meeting_note_preference
description: Meeting recap that contains one explicit user preference
content: Standup recap: Jordan shared the roadmap. I said I prefer we move standups to async going forward — I find them more distracting than useful.
criteria: At least 1 fact with category 'preference' describes the async-standup preference
No fact restates the meeting recap itself as a fact

name: task_with_context_clue
description: Task content that incidentally reveals the user's role
content: Draft CEO letter to investors — Q1 results. As CEO, this is one of my most important quarterly comms.
criteria: At least 1 fact with category 'context' identifies the user as CEO
No fact captures the draft-letter task itself as a user fact

name: meeting_relationship_mention
description: Meeting note that reveals a manager relationship
content: 1:1 with my manager Jordan. Discussed my promotion case. Jordan agreed to put me up for Director at next calibration.
criteria: At least 1 fact with category 'relationship' identifies Jordan as the user's manager
No fact captures the one-time promotion discussion as a durable fact

name: empty_entity
description: Minimal task content with no durable facts

name: task_with_habit
description: Task content that describes a recurring weekly routine
content: Friday shutdown routine: review the week, plan next week, clear inbox. Doing this every Friday at 4pm keeps me sane.
criteria: At least 1 fact with category 'habit' describes the Friday shutdown routine
No separate facts are extracted for the routine's sub-steps (review the week, plan next week, clear inbox)

name: meeting_note_multiple_facts
description: Onboarding meeting note for a new direct report
content: New hire onboarding: welcomed Priya Nair to the team. She's joining as Staff Engineer, reporting to me. She relocated from London to SF last month.
criteria: At least 1 fact with category 'relationship' identifies Priya as the user's direct report
No two facts share the same key

name: task_transient_only
description: Task content that is entirely transient
content: Buy birthday cake for Sam by Saturday.

name: meeting_note_preference_inferred
description: Meeting note where the user explicitly states a preference for daily standups
content: Retro with the team. I mentioned I don't like when we skip daily standups — they're my only way to see what's happening. Team agreed to keep them.
criteria: At least 1 fact with category 'preference' captures the value placed on daily standups
No fact captures the team's agreement or the retro itself as a durable fact

name: content_with_no_user_fact
description: Saved topical article with no user-specific information
content: Saved article: The state of AI coding assistants in 2026. Covers GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, and Claude Code. Claude Code leads in long-context tasks.
criteria: No fact is fabricated (no 'user prefers Claude Code' inferred from the user having saved the article)
